title: Delicious Peruvian Duck Arroz Con Pato
description: A traditional Peruvian dish made with duck, rice, and spices, served in a claypot. This charred and snappy meal is a great hangover cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the duck legs with peruvian spices, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in the claypot over medium heat and sear the duck legs until browned.
Add the chicken broth, char-grill-yang sauce, and crushed casabe to the claypot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the uncooked white rice to the claypot and stir to combine.
Cover the claypot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the claypot from the oven and stir in the chopped scallions.
Serve the arroz con pato hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
